articles.headline: PM Modi Inaugurates Navi Mumbai International Airport, Heralds New Era of Connectivity for Mumbai Metropolitan Region
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi officially inaugurated the Navi Mumbai International Airport (NMIA), marking a significant milestone in India's infrastructure development.
• The new airport is designed to alleviate congestion at the existing Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (CSMIA) and boost connectivity for the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.
• Developed under a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model, NMIA is expected to handle millions of passengers annually, enhancing trade and tourism.
• Its strategic location will foster economic growth in the Navi Mumbai region, attracting further investment and creating employment opportunities.
• The inauguration aligns with the government's vision for modernizing aviation infrastructure and positioning India as a global aviation hub.

articles.trivia: The idea for an alternative airport for Mumbai was first conceived in 1997 due to anticipated capacity constraints at the existing airport.

articles.headline: Chief Justice of India Attacked with Shoe Over 'Lord Vishnu Remarks'; Incident Raises Concerns on Judicial Security and Decorum
articles.explainer:
• Chief Justice of India B. R. Gavai was attacked by a shoe thrown by lawyer Rakesh Kishore during a public appearance, reportedly over remarks made by the CJI concerning Lord Vishnu.
• The incident has sparked widespread condemnation from the legal fraternity and political leaders, raising serious questions about judicial security and the decorum of public discourse.
• Authorities have initiated an investigation into the motive behind the attack and the security lapse that allowed the incident to occur.
• The event reignites debates on freedom of speech, contempt of court provisions, and the need for respectful engagement with judicial institutions.
• Calls for enhanced security measures for judges and a review of protocols for public interactions involving high constitutional functionaries are expected.

Role of civil society
Ethics in public life

articles.trivia: The Contempt of Courts Act, 1971, defines and limits the powers of certain courts in punishing contempt of court.

articles.headline: Devastating Landslide in Bilaspur, Himachal Pradesh Kills 15 as Bus Swept Away; Focus on Disaster Preparedness in Hilly Regions
articles.explainer:
• A tragic landslide in Bilaspur district, Himachal Pradesh, struck a moving bus, resulting in the death of 15 people and injuring several others.
• Heavy rainfall in the region over the past few days is believed to have triggered the landslide, highlighting the vulnerability of the Himalayan terrain.
• Rescue operations were immediately launched by NDRF and SDRF teams, facing challenging weather conditions and treacherous topography.
• The incident renews focus on the urgent need for robust disaster management strategies, early warning systems, and sustainable infrastructure development in ecologically fragile hilly areas.
• Experts call for stricter building codes, geological surveys, and community-level preparedness to mitigate the risks posed by natural calamities.

mainsPoints: Discuss the major anthropogenic and natural factors contributing to the increasing frequency and intensity of landslides in the Indian Himalayas.

articles.trivia: The Himalayas are considered one of the youngest and most tectonically active mountain ranges in the world, making them highly susceptible to landslides and earthquakes.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre on Expedited Implementation of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has issued directives to the Central Government, urging the expedited and comprehensive implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court underscored the fundamental importance of the Right to Privacy, reiterated in the Puttaswamy judgment, as a cornerstone of democratic freedoms in the digital age.
• Directives include setting up the Data Protection Board and framing necessary rules and regulations within a stipulated timeframe to ensure effective enforcement.
• Civil society groups and digital rights activists have welcomed the court's intervention, emphasizing the need for robust safeguards against data misuse by both state and private entities.
• The ruling is expected to accelerate the establishment of a strong data governance framework in India, impacting technology companies and citizens alike.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns; Focus on Supply-Side Measures for Price Stability
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, citing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food and energy prices.
• Despite signs of robust economic growth, the RBI emphasized a cautious approach to ensure price stability, aligning with its primary mandate.
• The central bank highlighted the need for coordinated fiscal and monetary policy actions, urging the government to implement supply-side measures to address inflation.
• Experts suggest that global geopolitical tensions and volatile commodity prices continue to pose risks to India's inflation outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while remaining vigilant on inflation, indicating a data-driven approach for future policy adjustments.

articles.headline: India and European Union Strengthen Partnership on Green Technologies and Critical Minerals at Bilateral Summit
articles.explainer:
• India and the European Union concluded a high-level bilateral summit, announcing enhanced cooperation in green technologies, renewable energy, and the critical minerals supply chain.
• The partnership aims to accelerate the transition to sustainable economies, fostering innovation and investment in areas like green hydrogen, electric mobility, and circular economy solutions.
• Both sides stressed the importance of diversifying supply chains for critical minerals, essential for high-tech industries and clean energy technologies, reducing reliance on single sources.
• The agreement includes provisions for joint research and development, technology transfer, and capacity building, emphasizing a shared commitment to climate action.
• This strategic collaboration underscores India's growing role in global climate initiatives and its efforts to build resilient economic partnerships.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite, Enhancing India's Remote Sensing Capabilities for Disaster Monitoring and Resource Management
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota, further bolstering India's remote sensing capabilities.
• The satellite, equipped with high-resolution sensors, will provide critical data for various applications, including disaster monitoring, agricultural assessment, urban planning, and water resource management.
• This mission underscores ISRO's commitment to indigenous space technology and its role in providing essential services for national development and security.
• The data generated by the EOS is expected to significantly enhance India's capacity for climate change studies and environmental monitoring.
• The launch is part of ISRO's ongoing efforts to build a robust constellation of satellites for comprehensive Earth observation.

articles.headline: Ministry of Health Unveils Revised Guidelines for National Policy on Rare Diseases, Aiming to Improve Access to Treatment and Support
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has released revised guidelines for the National Policy for Rare Diseases (NPRD), 2021, aiming to streamline treatment access and financial support.
• The updated policy focuses on strengthening the ecosystem for rare diseases, including early diagnosis, research, and indigenous drug development.
• Key changes include increasing financial assistance for treatment of specified rare diseases and expanding the network of Centers of Excellence (CoEs) across the country.
• Patient advocacy groups have largely welcomed the revisions, emphasizing the need for robust implementation and sustained funding for effective patient care.
• The policy seeks to address the significant challenges faced by patients with rare diseases, often characterized by high treatment costs and limited therapeutic options.

articles.headline: National Logistics Policy Shows Promising Gains in Reducing Costs and Improving Efficiency; Focus on Multimodal Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The National Logistics Policy (NLP), launched in 2022, is demonstrating promising results in streamlining India's logistics sector, with significant reductions in logistics costs.
• The policy's focus on multimodal connectivity, including integration of road, rail, air, and waterways, is enhancing efficiency and reducing transit times for goods.
• Key initiatives under the NLP, such as the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and PM Gati Shakti, are driving digital transformation and infrastructure development.
• Industry stakeholders report improved supply chain visibility, reduced inventory costs, and enhanced competitiveness for Indian businesses in domestic and international markets.
• The government aims to further reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, making India a more attractive destination for manufacturing and trade.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Intensifying Urban Heat Island Effect in Major Indian Cities, Urges Sustainable Urban Planning and Green Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by environmental scientists has revealed an intensifying Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in several major Indian cities, leading to significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
• The phenomenon is attributed to rapid urbanization, extensive use of heat-absorbing materials like concrete and asphalt, and reduced green cover.
• The report warns of severe public health implications, increased energy consumption for cooling, and exacerbated climate change impacts on urban populations.
• Experts advocate for urgent adoption of sustainable urban planning practices, including promoting green roofs, cool pavements, urban forestry, and efficient building designs.
• The findings underscore the critical need for integrating climate resilience into urban development policies to protect citizens and ensure livable cities.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Climate change
Important Geophysical phenomena
articles.source: Centre for Science and Environment (CSE) / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Report highlights intensifying Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in Indian cities.
Causes: rapid urbanization, heat-absorbing materials, reduced green cover.
Impacts: public health issues, increased energy consumption, exacerbated climate change.
Solutions: green roofs, cool pavements, urban forestry, efficient building designs.
UHI refers to cities being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect on the environment, public health, and energy demands in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Analyze various sustainable urban planning strategies and green infrastructure solutions that can effectively mitigate the UHI effect.
Examine the challenges in implementing climate-resilient urban development policies in India, considering rapid population growth and infrastructure needs.
Evaluate the role of local governments and community participation in fostering a 'cool city' approach and enhancing urban liveability.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a densely packed city with a red overlay, indicating higher temperatures compared to surrounding green areas.
articles.trivia: The term 'Urban Heat Island' was first documented in the early 19th century by Luke Howard, who observed London's warmer temperatures compared to its rural surroundings.

articles.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign to Boost Millets Cultivation and Promote 'Shree Anna' for Food Security and Nutritional Benefits
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government has launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign to significantly boost the cultivation and consumption of millets, rebranding them as 'Shree Anna'.
• The initiative aims to enhance food security, improve nutritional outcomes, and support farmers, especially in dryland regions, due to millets' climate-resilient properties.
• The campaign includes measures for increasing farmer awareness, providing subsidies for millet cultivation, and integrating millets into public distribution systems and school meal programs.
• Focus is also on developing value chains for millet products, encouraging processing, and promoting their health benefits to consumers.
• This push aligns with India's leadership in promoting the International Year of Millets (2023) and its commitment to sustainable agriculture.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for African Union's Digital Transformation Agenda, Emphasizes Sharing of Digital Public Infrastructure Models
articles.explainer:
• India has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting the African Union's (AU) digital transformation agenda, offering to share its expertise and successful models of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• During a high-level dialogue, India highlighted the potential of its 'India Stack' — a set of open APIs and digital public goods — to enable inclusive digital services in African nations.
• The collaboration aims to accelerate digital inclusion, foster innovation, and build resilient digital economies across the African continent.
• Areas of cooperation include digital identity, payments, data exchange, and open network for digital commerce, tailored to specific needs of AU member states.
• This initiative underscores India's 'South-South Cooperation' philosophy and its growing strategic partnership with Africa in emerging technologies.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms in Police Training and Modernization to Enhance Law Enforcement Effectiveness
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending comprehensive reforms in police training, infrastructure, and modernization to enhance the overall effectiveness of law enforcement agencies.
• The report highlighted critical gaps in current training modules, emphasizing the need for greater focus on cybercrime investigation, community policing, and sensitivity towards marginalized sections.
• Recommendations include increasing budgetary allocations for police modernization, upgrading technology, and improving living and working conditions for police personnel.
• The committee also stressed the importance of psychological training and stress management programs to address the mental health challenges faced by police forces.
• These reforms are crucial for improving public perception, accountability, and the capacity of the police to deal with evolving security threats and societal demands.

articles.headline: ASI Initiates Major Restoration Project for Ancient Buddhist Sites in Eastern Uttar Pradesh, Boosting Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a major restoration and conservation project for several ancient Buddhist sites in Eastern Uttar Pradesh.
• The initiative aims to preserve invaluable cultural heritage, enhance visitor experience, and boost heritage tourism in the region, particularly along the Buddhist Circuit.
• The project involves scientific conservation techniques, structural repairs, and landscaping, ensuring the long-term protection of these historically significant monuments.
• Local communities are expected to benefit from increased tourism through employment opportunities and development of supporting infrastructure.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Mixed Progress on SDG 4, Calls for Enhanced Investment in Early Childhood Education and Digital Literacy
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released its latest report on India's progress towards Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4) – Quality Education, indicating mixed results across various indicators.
• While significant strides have been made in increasing enrollment rates at primary levels, challenges persist in ensuring equitable access, improving learning outcomes, and enhancing digital literacy.
• The report calls for enhanced public and private investment in early childhood education, teacher training, and technology integration to bridge existing gaps.
• It emphasizes the need for a holistic approach, aligning with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, to ensure inclusive and equitable quality education for all.
• The findings will serve as a crucial input for policymakers to refine strategies and accelerate progress towards achieving SDG 4 targets by 2030.

articles.headline: India Participates in Antarctic Treaty Consultative Meeting, Emphasizes Environmental Protection and Scientific Research
articles.explainer:
• India actively participated in the annual Antarctic Treaty Consultative Meeting (ATCM), reaffirming its commitment to environmental protection and peaceful scientific research in the Antarctic region.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its ongoing scientific expeditions to its research stations, Maitri and Bharati, focusing on climate change, glaciology, and polar biology.
• Discussions at the ATCM included strengthening conservation measures, regulating tourism, and addressing emerging challenges such as plastic pollution and climate change impacts on the polar ice caps.
• India emphasized the importance of international cooperation and adherence to the principles of the Antarctic Treaty System to ensure the continent remains a demilitarized zone dedicated to science.
• The participation underscores India's growing scientific capabilities and its responsible role in global environmental governance.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Information Infrastructure Following Recent Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government has announced enhanced measures to bolster the cybersecurity framework protecting Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) across various sectors.
• This move comes in response to recent reports of sophisticated cyberattacks and espionage attempts targeting essential services and government networks.
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is collaborating with sector-specific agencies to conduct comprehensive audits and implement advanced threat detection systems.
• Focus areas include strengthening incident response mechanisms, promoting information sharing among stakeholders, and enhancing the skills of cybersecurity professionals.
• The initiative aims to safeguard vital national assets, maintain operational continuity, and protect citizen data from evolving cyber threats.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on AI Ethics and Governance Framework for Responsible AI Adoption in India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has published a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance in India.
• The paper emphasizes the need for responsible AI adoption, focusing on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy to mitigate potential risks.
• It seeks to foster innovation while ensuring that AI development and deployment align with societal values and constitutional principles.
• The framework proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society in shaping AI policies and regulations.
• This initiative is crucial for positioning India as a leader in ethical AI and ensuring that AI technologies contribute positively to sustainable development.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Navi Mumbai International Airport (NMIA):
1. It was inaugurated by the Chief Minister of Maharashtra.
2. It is developed under a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
3. Its primary goal is to serve as a cargo-only airport for the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Navi Mumbai International Airport was inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Statement 2 is correct: NMIA is developed under a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model. Statement 3 is incorrect: Its primary goal is to alleviate passenger congestion at the existing Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport and boost overall connectivity for the region, handling both passenger and cargo traffic.

prelimsMocks.question:
The recent shoe-throwing incident involving the Chief Justice of India B. R. Gavai raises concerns related to which of the following aspects of the Indian polity?
1. Judicial independence
2. Contempt of Court
3. Freedom of Speech
4. Judicial accountability
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The incident involving the CJI touches upon all these aspects. It questions judicial independence by attacking the institution, potentially falls under contempt of court, sparks debate on the limits of freedom of speech, and implicitly raises questions about judicial accountability in public perception, even if the act itself is deplorable.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, is/are correct?
1. It was passed by the Indian Parliament in 2023.
2. The Supreme Court's K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a statutory right, which the DPDP Act aims to protect.
3. The Act mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The DPDP Act was passed in 2023. Statement 2 is incorrect: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right, not a statutory right. Statement 3 is correct: The Act mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Shree Anna', recently in news, is associated with which of the following?
options: (a) A new variety of high-yielding paddy developed by ICAR
(b) A government initiative to promote organic farming in North-Eastern states
(c) The rebranding of millets to boost their cultivation and consumption
(d) A scheme for providing fortified rice through the Public Distribution System
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) The rebranding of millets to boost their cultivation and consumption
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indian government has launched a nationwide campaign to significantly boost the cultivation and consumption of millets, rebranding them as 'Shree Anna' to highlight their nutritional and climate-resilient benefits.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in cities is primarily caused by which of the following?
1. Reduced green cover and vegetation
2. Extensive use of heat-absorbing materials like concrete and asphalt
3. Increased industrial emissions and vehicular pollution
4. Presence of tall buildings trapping heat
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The UHI effect is primarily a result of urban physical characteristics. Reduced green cover (1), heat-absorbing materials (2), and tall buildings (4) that block wind and trap heat are direct causes. While increased emissions (3) contribute to overall global warming and urban air pollution, they are not the primary cause of the 'island' effect where urban areas are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to physical changes in the land surface.

GS1.headline: Devastating Landslide in Bilaspur, Himachal Pradesh Kills 15 as Bus Swept Away; Focus on Disaster Preparedness in Hilly Regions
GS1.explainer:
• A tragic landslide in Bilaspur district, Himachal Pradesh, struck a moving bus, resulting in the death of 15 people and injuring several others.
• Heavy rainfall in the region over the past few days is believed to have triggered the landslide, highlighting the vulnerability of the Himalayan terrain.
• Rescue operations were immediately launched by NDRF and SDRF teams, facing challenging weather conditions and treacherous topography.
• The incident renews focus on the urgent need for robust disaster management strategies, early warning systems, and sustainable infrastructure development in ecologically fragile hilly areas.
• Experts call for stricter building codes, geological surveys, and community-level preparedness to mitigate the risks posed by natural calamities.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Intensifying Urban Heat Island Effect in Major Indian Cities, Urges Sustainable Urban Planning and Green Infrastructure
GS1.explainer:
• A recent report by environmental scientists has revealed an intensifying Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in several major Indian cities, leading to significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
• The phenomenon is attributed to rapid urbanization, extensive use of heat-absorbing materials like concrete and asphalt, and reduced green cover.
• The report warns of severe public health implications, increased energy consumption for cooling, and exacerbated climate change impacts on urban populations.
• Experts advocate for urgent adoption of sustainable urban planning practices, including promoting green roofs, cool pavements, urban forestry, and efficient building designs.
• The findings underscore the critical need for integrating climate resilience into urban development policies to protect citizens and ensure livable cities.

Climate change
Important Geophysical phenomena

GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a major restoration and conservation project for several ancient Buddhist sites in Eastern Uttar Pradesh.
• The initiative aims to preserve invaluable cultural heritage, enhance visitor experience, and boost heritage tourism in the region, particularly along the Buddhist Circuit.
• The project involves scientific conservation techniques, structural repairs, and landscaping, ensuring the long-term protection of these historically significant monuments.
• Local communities are expected to benefit from increased tourism through employment opportunities and development of supporting infrastructure.
• This effort aligns with India's commitment to protecting its rich archaeological legacy and promoting cultural diplomacy.

GS2.headline: Chief Justice of India Attacked with Shoe Over 'Lord Vishnu Remarks'; Incident Raises Concerns on Judicial Security and Decorum
GS2.explainer:
• Chief Justice of India B. R. Gavai was attacked by a shoe thrown by lawyer Rakesh Kishore during a public appearance, reportedly over remarks made by the CJI concerning Lord Vishnu.
• The incident has sparked widespread condemnation from the legal fraternity and political leaders, raising serious questions about judicial security and the decorum of public discourse.
• Authorities have initiated an investigation into the motive behind the attack and the security lapse that allowed the incident to occur.
• The event reignites debates on freedom of speech, contempt of court provisions, and the need for respectful engagement with judicial institutions.
• Calls for enhanced security measures for judges and a review of protocols for public interactions involving high constitutional functionaries are expected.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre on Expedited Implementation of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Emphasizes Right to Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has issued directives to the Central Government, urging the expedited and comprehensive implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The court underscored the fundamental importance of the Right to Privacy, reiterated in the Puttaswamy judgment, as a cornerstone of democratic freedoms in the digital age.
• Directives include setting up the Data Protection Board and framing necessary rules and regulations within a stipulated timeframe to ensure effective enforcement.
• Civil society groups and digital rights activists have welcomed the court's intervention, emphasizing the need for robust safeguards against data misuse by both state and private entities.
• The ruling is expected to accelerate the establishment of a strong data governance framework in India, impacting technology companies and citizens alike.

• Both sides stressed the importance of diversifying supply chains for critical minerals, essential for high-tech industries and clean energy technologies, reducing reliance on single sources.
• The agreement includes provisions for joint research and development, technology transfer, and capacity building, emphasizing a shared commitment to climate action.
• This strategic collaboration underscores India's growing role in global climate initiatives and its efforts to build resilient economic partnerships.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Health Unveils Revised Guidelines for National Policy on Rare Diseases, Aiming to Improve Access to Treatment and Support

• The updated policy focuses on strengthening the ecosystem for rare diseases, including early diagnosis, research, and indigenous drug development.
• Key changes include increasing financial assistance for treatment of specified rare diseases and expanding the network of Centers of Excellence (CoEs) across the country.
• Patient advocacy groups have largely welcomed the revisions, emphasizing the need for robust implementation and sustained funding for effective patient care.
• The policy seeks to address the significant challenges faced by patients with rare diseases, often characterized by high treatment costs and limited therapeutic options.

GS2.headline: India Participates in Antarctic Treaty Consultative Meeting, Emphasizes Environmental Protection and Scientific Research
GS2.explainer:
• India actively participated in the annual Antarctic Treaty Consultative Meeting (ATCM), reaffirming its commitment to environmental protection and peaceful scientific research in the Antarctic region.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its ongoing scientific expeditions to its research stations, Maitri and Bharati, focusing on climate change, glaciology, and polar biology.
• Discussions at the ATCM included strengthening conservation measures, regulating tourism, and addressing emerging challenges such as plastic pollution and climate change impacts on the polar ice caps.
• India emphasized the importance of international cooperation and adherence to the principles of the Antarctic Treaty System to ensure the continent remains a demilitarized zone dedicated to science.
• The participation underscores India's growing scientific capabilities and its responsible role in global environmental governance.

GS2.trivia: India became a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty in 1983 and was accorded the status of Consultative Party in the same year, allowing it to participate in decision-making.

GS3.headline: PM Modi Inaugurates Navi Mumbai International Airport, Heralds New Era of Connectivity for Mumbai Metropolitan Region
GS3.explainer:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi officially inaugurated the Navi Mumbai International Airport (NMIA), marking a significant milestone in India's infrastructure development.
• The new airport is designed to alleviate congestion at the existing Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (CSMIA) and boost connectivity for the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.
• Developed under a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model, NMIA is expected to handle millions of passengers annually, enhancing trade and tourism.
• Its strategic location will foster economic growth in the Navi Mumbai region, attracting further investment and creating employment opportunities.
• The inauguration aligns with the government's vision for modernizing aviation infrastructure and positioning India as a global aviation hub.

GS3.trivia: The idea for an alternative airport for Mumbai was first conceived in 1997 due to anticipated capacity constraints at the existing airport.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns; Focus on Supply-Side Measures for Price Stability
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, citing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food and energy prices.
• Despite signs of robust economic growth, the RBI emphasized a cautious approach to ensure price stability, aligning with its primary mandate.
• The central bank highlighted the need for coordinated fiscal and monetary policy actions, urging the government to implement supply-side measures to address inflation.
• Experts suggest that global geopolitical tensions and volatile commodity prices continue to pose risks to India's inflation outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while remaining vigilant on inflation, indicating a data-driven approach for future policy adjustments.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite, Enhancing India's Remote Sensing Capabilities for Disaster Monitoring and Resource Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota, further bolstering India's remote sensing capabilities.
• The satellite, equipped with high-resolution sensors, will provide critical data for various applications, including disaster monitoring, agricultural assessment, urban planning, and water resource management.
• This mission underscores ISRO's commitment to indigenous space technology and its role in providing essential services for national development and security.
• The data generated by the EOS is expected to significantly enhance India's capacity for climate change studies and environmental monitoring.
• The launch is part of ISRO's ongoing efforts to build a robust constellation of satellites for comprehensive Earth observation.

GS3.headline: National Logistics Policy Shows Promising Gains in Reducing Costs and Improving Efficiency; Focus on Multimodal Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The National Logistics Policy (NLP), launched in 2022, is demonstrating promising results in streamlining India's logistics sector, with significant reductions in logistics costs.
• The policy's focus on multimodal connectivity, including integration of road, rail, air, and waterways, is enhancing efficiency and reducing transit times for goods.
• Key initiatives under the NLP, such as the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) and PM Gati Shakti, are driving digital transformation and infrastructure development.
• Industry stakeholders report improved supply chain visibility, reduced inventory costs, and enhanced competitiveness for Indian businesses in domestic and international markets.
• The government aims to further reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, making India a more attractive destination for manufacturing and trade.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Campaign to Boost Millets Cultivation and Promote 'Shree Anna' for Food Security and Nutritional Benefits
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government has launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign to significantly boost the cultivation and consumption of millets, rebranding them as 'Shree Anna'.
• The initiative aims to enhance food security, improve nutritional outcomes, and support farmers, especially in dryland regions, due to millets' climate-resilient properties.
• The campaign includes measures for increasing farmer awareness, providing subsidies for millet cultivation, and integrating millets into public distribution systems and school meal programs.
• Focus is also on developing value chains for millet products, encouraging processing, and promoting their health benefits to consumers.
• This push aligns with India's leadership in promoting the International Year of Millets (2023) and its commitment to sustainable agriculture.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Information Infrastructure Following Recent Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government has announced enhanced measures to bolster the cybersecurity framework protecting Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) across various sectors.
• This move comes in response to recent reports of sophisticated cyberattacks and espionage attempts targeting essential services and government networks.
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is collaborating with sector-specific agencies to conduct comprehensive audits and implement advanced threat detection systems.
• Focus areas include strengthening incident response mechanisms, promoting information sharing among stakeholders, and enhancing the skills of cybersecurity professionals.
• The initiative aims to safeguard vital national assets, maintain operational continuity, and protect citizen data from evolving cyber threats.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on AI Ethics and Governance Framework for Responsible AI Adoption in India
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has published a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance in India.
• The paper emphasizes the need for responsible AI adoption, focusing on principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy to mitigate potential risks.
• It seeks to foster innovation while ensuring that AI development and deployment align with societal values and constitutional principles.
• The framework proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society in shaping AI policies and regulations.
• This initiative is crucial for positioning India as a leader in ethical AI and ensuring that AI technologies contribute positively to sustainable development.
